mission.title: Install and Configure Bpfman on Kubernetes
mission.description: bpfman is an eBPF Manager designed to simplify the deployment and administration of eBPF programs in Kubernetes environments. Installing bpfman allows you to efficiently manage eBPF programs, providing insights and control over their usage in your system.

steps.title: Add the bpfman Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official bpfman Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add bpfman https://bpfman.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install bpfman using Helm
steps.description:
Install bpfman in the 'bpfman' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install bpfman bpfman/bpfman --namespace bpfman --create-namespace --version v0.5.6
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the bpfman pods are running successfully in the 'bpfman' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bpfman
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the bpfman deployment to set resource limits for better performance and stability.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
# Add the following lines under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Access the bpfman dashboard
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the bpfman dashboard locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/bpfman --namespace bpfman 8080:80
# Now access the dashboard at http://localhost:8080
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of bpfman will show the bpfman pods running in the 'bpfman' namespace. You can access the bpfman dashboard via port forwarding, allowing you to manage your eBPF programs effectively.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bpfman https://bpfman.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install bpfman bpfman/bpfman --namespace bpfman --create-namespace --version v0.5.6

kubectl edit deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
# Add the following lines under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

kubectl port-forward service/bpfman --namespace bpfman 8080:80
# Now access the dashboard at http://localhost:8080

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the bpfman release from the 'bpfman' namespace using Helm. This will remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall bpfman --namespace bpfman
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources that were created by bpfman. This ensures that all related resources are removed from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd bpfman.bpfman.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace bpfman
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'bpfman' namespace to clean up any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace bpfman
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to backup the current state of your bpfman installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
helm get values bpfman --namespace bpfman > bpfman-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and then upgrade bpfman to the latest version. Make sure to specify the version you want to upgrade to.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade bpfman bpfman/bpfman --namespace bpfman --version v0.6.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check that the bpfman pods are running the new version and are in a healthy state.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bpfman
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace bpfman
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the bpfman pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors that might indicate the cause of the crash.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace bpfman
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the bpfman dashboard, ensure that the service is running and the port forwarding is set up correctly. Check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace bpfman
kubectl port-forward service/bpfman --namespace bpfman 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to resource limits, check the resource usage of the bpfman pods and adjust the limits if necessary.
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace bpfman
kubectl edit deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive errors related to missing CRDs, ensure that the CRDs were not deleted accidentally. You can recreate them if necessary.
```bash
kubectl get crd
helm install bpfman bpfman/bpfman --namespace bpfman --create-namespace --version v0.5.6
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
